symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe dysphagia to solids after eating dry crackers

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden dysphagia while swallowing a solid is the classic bolus-impaction history.
Dry, low-lubrication food adheres to dry mucosa and produces functional dysphagia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense burning/raw throat pain (odynophagia) on swallowing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucosal abrasion of dry pharyngeal tissue causes burning discomfort.
Sore, burning throat is non-specific and overlaps with pharyngitis.
A subjective choking/foreign-body sensation cannot be dismissed remotely and flags possible obstruction.
Distress can amplify a foreign-body throat sensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound dry mouth with minimal saliva (sicca)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyposalivation is the mechanistic basis for dry-food adherence and mucosal friction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Retained ability to swallow saliva and small sips of water

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Passing saliva and clear fluids argues against a high-grade complete obstruction, but a partial bolus remains possible.

symptoms.symptom_name: No lip/tongue swelling, no wheeze, no breathing difficulty (patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of angioedema features lowers the probability but does not exclude an early/evolving reaction remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: High-throat (not retrosternal) localisation of the sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pharyngeal (high) localisation is less typical of a lower oesophageal bolus, but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry, erythematous oropharyngeal mucosa without exudate; no saliva pooling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician finding confirming dry mucosa; absence of exudate argues against bacterial pharyngitis.
No tonsillar exudate lowers the likelihood of streptococcal pharyngitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patent airway; no stridor; no uvular oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician finding excluding airway oedema — unavailable via telehealth.
A fully patent airway on inspection reduces the immediate airway threat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Comfortable supervised 30 mL water swallow without cough or regurgitation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed supervised swallow test that materially excludes a significant bolus — the single finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.
Afebrile with no tender nodes argues strongly against streptococcal pharyngitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs including SpO2 100%, RR 16 (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal saturation and respiratory rate argue against significant airway/respiratory compromise; clinician-measured and sealed.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden dysphagia and burning on a dry-food trigger in a patient with profound hyposalivation points to mucosal friction rather than obstruction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person oropharyngeal examination and airway patency assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive step to exclude food-bolus impaction and angioedema; cannot be performed over telehealth.
Objectively excludes a significant retained bolus.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: ENT review / flexible nasendoscopy or oesophagoscopy if a bolus is suspected on assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Escalation pathway if in-person assessment cannot exclude impaction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical throat swab / routine bloods at this acute presentation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Afebrile with no exudate and no infective prodrome — over-investigation not indicated acutely; the priority is excluding obstruction by examination.
